title: parts
description: Minimal set of parts needed to build a URDF for the input object.

object.description: Snake_case name of the assembled object (e.g. french_door_refrigerator, oven, laptop, stapler).

name.description: Short, unique snake_case part name used as the image/GLB file stem. Must clearly identify the component — not a vague label like 'body' or 'part_1'.

description.description: One sentence identifying WHAT the part is and WHERE it sits on the object. Used as the image-generation prompt — describe only what is visually isolatable. Do not repeat geometry data already in size_fraction or position_in_parent.

parent.description: Name of the parent part, or null for the single root/base part.

joint_type.description: URDF joint type connecting this part to its parent.

world_dims.description: REQUIRED for the root part only. Real-world [width_m, depth_m, height_m] in metres, estimated from the source image. Optional on non-root parts to override size_fraction with exact world dimensions.

size_fraction.description: REQUIRED for every non-root part. Estimated size of this part as a fraction of its PARENT's world dimensions: [width_fraction, depth_fraction, height_fraction]. Each value is relative to the parent — 1.0 means the same size as the parent along that axis. Values > 1 are allowed if the part extends beyond the parent. Examples: a door that covers half the parent width and 65% of its height with thin depth → [0.5, 0.06, 0.65]; a full-width full-depth bottom drawer at 35% height → [1.0, 1.0, 0.35]; a laptop screen same footprint as base, very thin → [1.0, 0.03, 0.90].

position_in_parent.description: REQUIRED for every non-root part. Where this part sits inside the parent, using one or two keywords. Horizontal (X-axis): 'left' (part occupies left side), 'center-left', 'center', 'center-right', 'right'. Vertical (Z-axis): 'bottom', 'lower', 'middle', 'upper', 'top'. Combine with a dash: 'bottom-center', 'upper-left', 'top-right'. Use 'center' when the part is centered in both X and Z (e.g. a turntable). For doors: 'left' or 'right'. For drawers: 'bottom' or 'bottom-center'. For lids: 'top'. The Y (depth) position is derived automatically from hinge_side and slide_axis.

hinge_side.description: REQUIRED for revolute joints. The physical EDGE of this part where the hinge/pivot is attached. 'left': hinge on the part's left vertical edge — door swings open rightward/inward via Z-rotation (e.g. a left fridge door, a left cabinet door). 'right': hinge on right vertical edge — door swings open leftward via Z-rotation (e.g. a right fridge door). 'bottom': hinge on the BOTTOM horizontal edge — top of part swings forward via X-rotation (e.g. oven door, dishwasher door, laptop screen relative to base). 'top': hinge on the TOP horizontal edge — bottom of part swings open via X-rotation (e.g. a chest freezer lid, a car hood that opens backward). Omit for non-revolute joints.

slide_axis.description: REQUIRED for prismatic joints. The world-space direction the part moves when it opens or extends. Blender world axes: X = left(−)/right(+), Y = back(+)/front(−), Z = down(−)/up(+). '-y': slides toward the viewer / forward — the default for kitchen drawers, desk drawers. '+z': slides upward — e.g. an oven rack being lifted out. '-z': slides downward. '+x': slides to the right. '-x': slides to the left. '+y': slides backward / away from viewer (rare). Omit for non-prismatic joints.

open_angle_deg.description: REQUIRED for revolute joints. How many degrees the part is open in the source image (0 = fully closed, 90 = perpendicular, etc.). Omit for non-revolute joints.

pullout_fraction.description: REQUIRED for prismatic joints. How far the part is extended in the source image as a fraction of its full travel along slide_axis (0 = fully closed, 0.5 = half extended, 1.0 = fully extended). Omit for non-prismatic joints.
